briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for April 7, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Urgent Warning: Energy Crisis from the Middle East and Deepening Reliance on Fossil Fuels
sections.content: According to news released today, rising fossil fuel prices due to the Iran war are stimulating a significant increase in fossil fuel production, allowing major polluting nations to profit from high fossil fuel prices. Current oil prices are approximately $110 per barrel, with some forecasts predicting they could reach $150 [1, 147]. This energy shock originating from the Middle East has put Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) at risk of doubling its debt, emphasizing the urgency of transitioning to renewable energy to address the energy crisis [96, 114, 116, 120, 122]. Furthermore, concerns are being raised that carbon neutrality goals could be pushed back as big tech companies are shifting towards gas power plants due to the need to secure AI power [27]. Some also criticize the government for only 'verbally' promoting energy transition, supporting the oil industry with 10 trillion won while investing only a small amount in renewable energy [100]. This clearly reveals global reliance on fossil fuels and energy security vulnerabilities, indicating the need for immediate action.

sections.title: ⚠️ Deepening Impacts of Climate Change and Economic Losses
sections.content: The direct impacts of climate change are progressively intensifying both domestically and internationally. Domestically, in the Chungnam region, climate change has caused an emergency in Liriope muscari production, necessitating the development of new varieties [2, 10, 11]. Internationally, allergy seasons are lengthening across the United States, and pollen seasons are intensifying worldwide, escalating the health impacts of climate change to the extent of being considered a public health emergency [136, 139, 145]. Frontline communities have reported extreme heat and power outages due to the impacts of climate change, along with the general phenomenon of increasingly dangerous wildfire seasons [133, 134]. Economic losses due to climate change are increasing annually, leading to growing calls to fill the coverage gaps in climate insurance [8].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating the Great Transition to Renewable Energy and Policy Efforts
sections.content: The government has announced its plan to expand the share of renewable energy generation to over 20% by 2030 and achieve the 100GW target ahead of schedule, declaring a 'Great Energy Transition' centered on renewable energy [80, 83, 86, 91, 93, 107, 108, 119]. Various organizations, including the Korea Wind Energy Industry Association, have welcomed the government's energy transition plan and expressed their anticipation for the expansion of renewable energy [94, 99, 123, 126]. A key strategy seminar on ESS (Energy Storage Systems) and BESS (Battery Energy Storage Systems) was held to address the intermittency issues of renewable energy [101], and various local governments, including Pyeongchang County and Incheon Jung-gu, are actively promoting the adoption of renewable energy by supporting new and renewable energy facilities and pursuing convergence projects [102, 103, 110, 121]. China's ability to avoid the impact of the Middle East oil shock through self-reliance in electric vehicles and renewable energy demonstrates the positive impact of renewable energy transition on national economic security [104, 112, 125].

sections.title: 🌱 Spreading Efforts for Carbon Neutrality and Climate Adaptation Domestically and Internationally
sections.content: Various efforts for carbon neutrality and climate change adaptation are expanding both domestically and internationally. Yeosu City established a safety management system in conjunction with the UN Climate Change Convention's Climate Week [3, 13], and Busan City is investing 13.9 billion won to create 11 urban forests, including Haeundae Arboretum, thereby strengthening its climate change response capabilities [4, 12, 18]. Paju City opened 'Eco-On Environmental Center,' which will serve as an integrated hub for environmental education and carbon neutrality practices [20, 64, 70], and Incheon Gyeyang-gu is operating 'Eco-School' for children to practice carbon neutrality [15]. Various local governments, including Gwacheon City, Siheung City, and Gyeongbuk Province, are recruiting participants for the automobile carbon neutrality point system, offering incentives of up to 100,000 won to citizens who reduce their mileage [21, 31, 36, 38, 44, 48, 55, 61, 67, 68, 77], and Jeju Province is expanding support for hydrogen vehicle purchases to pursue carbon neutrality [30]. Internationally, Africa is making efforts to secure sustainability through agricultural innovation and smart city development amidst the climate change crisis [17], and the possibility that beavers could be nature's secret weapon against climate change has also been raised [144].

sections.title: 📰 Active International Cooperation and Discussions for Climate Change Response
sections.content: International cooperation and discussions for climate change response are actively continuing. The Bank of Korea and the Banque de France held a joint seminar on digital assets and climate change, emphasizing the importance of this field [5, 7, 9, 14, 16, 143]. Domestically, the Presidential Committee on Carbon Neutrality and Green Growth is playing a role in responding to the climate crisis [23, 33, 35, 37], and the Ministry of Climate, Energy, and Environment is strengthening its policy implementation capabilities, including key personnel appointments such as the Deputy Head of the Solar Power Dissemination Promotion Team [131]. Internationally, the state of Virginia is seeking to rejoin a controversial climate change agreement by July 1 [132], and a meeting of the Water Group members of the New South Wales (NSW) Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment, and Water was held, emphasizing the importance of feedback [146].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing demonstrates that various efforts, both domestic and international, are continuing to transition to renewable energy and achieve carbon neutrality, even amidst the threats of climate change. We hope these efforts will bear fruit and lead to a sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: The government's 'Energy Transition Plan' aims to expand renewable energy to over 20% by 2030, achieve 100GW supply, and mandate solar panels on factory roofs, including 'sunlight, wind, and grid income' for 10 million citizens. This plan, which shifts the energy security concept from fossil fuels to renewables and seeks to reduce Middle East dependency, is drawing criticism regarding its feasibility, particularly on producing naphtha from renewable sources. Regional electricity price differentiation is also being considered.

summary.phenomenon: The government is promoting an 'Energy Transition Plan' to expand the share of renewable energy to over 20% by 2030, aiming for 100GW of renewable energy supply, and including measures such as mandatory solar panels on factory roofs. This plan also encompasses realizing 'sunlight, wind, and grid income' for 10 million citizens.
summary.cause: This initiative aims to shift the concept of energy security from fossil fuels to renewable energy and reduce reliance on Middle Eastern energy.
summary.outlook: While the transition to a renewable energy-centric system is expected to accelerate, criticism is being raised regarding the feasibility of the plan, such as the possibility of producing naphtha from renewable energy. Regional differentiation in electricity prices may also be pursued.

articles.description: Worst polluters hold world’s future in their hands as they benefit from higher fossil fuel prices, but global trends favour renewablesOil stands at about $110 a barrel and some forecasts have predicted it could reach $150. Food prices are on the rise and are expected to leap further owing to the fertiliser supply crunch, leading the World Food Programme USA to warn that global food insecurity could reach record levels, with 45 million more people pushed into acute hunger. summary.phenomenon: The Iran war has exposed global reliance on fossil fuels, allowing major polluters to benefit from high fossil fuel prices. Currently, oil prices are approximately $110 per barrel, with some forecasts predicting they could reach $150.
summary.cause: The Iran war exposed global reliance on fossil fuels and caused oil prices to rise.
summary.outlook: Food prices are rising and are expected to surge further due to fertilizer supply shortages, leading the World Food Programme USA to warn that global food insecurity could reach record levels, with over 45 million more people pushed into acute hunger.
